purpose: CORRECTED re-read of FB1 for Rapi: Red Hood, applying the corrected understanding that the red box RIGHT of the crosshair is a ROCKET METER (0-100%, fills & resets) and NOT boss HP. Supersedes the FB1/meter portions of docs/probe-data/rrh-control-recon.json (2026-07-15), which mis-read both.

video.focus_unit: Rapi: Red Hood (slug rapi-red-hood); per owner all rendered popups are hers
video.boss: neutral, no boss HP bar in this mode
video.note_timebase: All times below are VIDEO seconds. Fight timer at video 14.0s reads 02:54 (=6s elapsed). FB1 yellow FULL-BURST splash and batch are at ~14.2-14.4s video.

headline_corrections_vs_prior_recon: Right red '%' box is the ROCKET METER (0-100%), confirmed by CLIMBING + RESETTING readings (6->10->15->30 ... ->70, then reset 65->34). Prior recon called it boss HP 'DRL ROY' reading 097->094 decreasing; that is wrong for the combat window read here.
FB1 dominant popup = 25,319,690 (EIGHT digits, ~25.3M), NOT 2,319,690 (~2.3M) as prior recon read. Re-verified digit-by-digit across frames 874-886: '2 5 3 1 9 6 9 0'. This is a ~10x correction.

FB1 is the LARGEST batch of the fight, NOT the smallest. It follows the longest pre-burst rocket-accumulation window (~14s from battle start). Later FBs are equal or smaller (FB3 batch ~17-27M, FB2 much smaller).

fb1_batch_popup.color: PLAIN WHITE with black outline (NOT red)
fb1_batch_popup.core_label: NONE - no red 'CORE HIT' label above or on it
fb1_batch_popup.position: dead-center in the reticle/crosshair on the boss body, inside a red radial explosion effect
fb1_batch_popup.lifetime: single lingering popup visible ~frames 864-892 (14.4-14.87s); value constant frame-to-frame = one popup, not a changing counter
fb1_batch_popup.confidence_noncore: high for THIS consolidated popup (plainly white, no label)

explosion_core_fraction.resolved: 0 of 1 (the consolidated FB1-start batch popup is plain/non-core)
explosion_core_fraction.caveat: This is the CONSOLIDATED batch popup. Whether the underlying individual rockets each core cannot be determined, because the batch renders as ONE number, not N separable instances. So this neither confirms nor cleanly refutes 'explosions core' - it shows the consolidated FB-start popup rendering non-core.

resolvable_instances_in_fb1_batch.explanation: The accumulated-rocket batch renders as a SINGLE consolidated popup (25,319,690), not as N separate per-rocket popups. During the ~0.15s FULL-BURST yellow swipe (frames 850-864) there is a tightly stacked vertical column of pink numbers, but those are the ramping NORMAL-FIRE cores (~23k-30k mid-ramp), not the explosions - they overlap inseparably. The explosion itself resolves to the one 25.3M popup.
resolvable_instances_in_fb1_batch.per_rocket_value_inferred: IF per-rocket explosion is ~2-4.5M (owner's prior-context expectation), the 25.3M batch implies ~6-12 rockets accumulated pre-FB1. Cross-checks: 25.3M / normal-core(83.7k) ~= 302x total; per-rocket 2.8-4.2M would be ~33-50x a normal core, matching the ~30-50x ratio owner expected. Confidence: low-medium (derived, not measured).

requirement_2_rocket_meter.identity: Right-of-crosshair red '%' box = rocket meter, 0-100%, fills with normal attacks and RESETS at 100 (rocket fires). Confirmed by resets observed.
resets_observed: between 11.2s(30%) and 13.0s(18%): filled to 100 & reset once (outside burst)

fill_rate_estimate.outside_burst_early: ~20%/s (t=10-11.2s) -> ~5s per rocket; rate rises as fire rate/buffs ramp
fill_rate_estimate.burst_vs_outside_ratio: meter fills roughly 2x+ faster inside her FB, qualitatively consistent with the owner's 120-shots-outside / 60-shots-inside assumption (also compounded by higher in-FB fire rate)

requirement_2_rocket_meter.shots_per_fill_direct_count: NOT measurable - individual shots fire far too fast to count against the overlap; only the % box and its resets are readable.
requirement_2_rocket_meter.rockets_between_fb1_and_fb2: NOT directly countable. Estimated from the FB2 batch magnitude instead (see requirement_4); FB2's batch is much smaller than FB1's, implying FEWER rockets accumulated in the short inter-burst gap than in the ~14s pre-FB1 window.
requirement_2_rocket_meter.left_crosshair_box: Separate white/red box LEFT of crosshair = her AMMO (e.g. 293->265 firing, reload jumps up, 121->080 during FB = ~58 rounds/s in-FB fire rate). At a rocket launch it flashes red '100' with 'PREP FOR LAUNCH!' / 'SVD-DD READY' / 'DRL RDY' banners. Not the rocket %-meter.
requirement_2_rocket_meter.confidence: meter identity high; precise shots-per-fill cadence low (sparse sampling, uncountable shots)

requirement_3_normal_core_reference.normal_shot_cores_during_fb1: RED 'CORE HIT' popups, stable value ~83,708 (repeated), plus a higher variant ~100,449 (crit-core / buffed). These match the owner's expected ~84-94k normal-shot core.
requirement_3_normal_core_reference.normal_hits_pre_burst_ramp: white plain ~7,056 and ~8,537 (early, before buffs ramp), orange crit ~12,806. These ramp up to the ~83.7k cores by mid-FB as Crown/LM/Helm buffs stack.
requirement_3_normal_core_reference.distinguishing_rule: An EXPLOSION popup (25.3M consolidated) is ~300x a single normal core (83.7k) and appears at the reticle/body center as plain white; a normal-shot core is red 'CORE HIT' ~84k up on the boss core. They are unmistakable by size.

requirement_4_other_fb_batches.counter_caveat: Top DAMAGE counter is TEAM TOTAL (per prior recon: sum of all 4 units = 2,061,678,629 at end), so batch deltas include allies' FB-start damage, not Rapi alone.

fb1_batch.batch_attributable: ~25.3M, matching the single 25,319,690 popup nearly exactly
fb1_batch.note: Under 'only Rapi renders', the 25.3M popup is Rapi's; the near-equal team delta is consistent with her batch dominating that instant, though the team-total counter cannot itself prove attribution vs an ally nuke (e.g. Little Mermaid).

fb2_batch.observation: much smaller / smeared; 'RELOADING...' visible at 28.3s and the launch banner ('PREP FOR LAUNCH!', meter 84%) implies the FB2 rocket fires slightly later. A ~1,195,658 popup (candidate single in-FB rocket explosion, plain/white) was visible at 28.3s.
fb2_batch.batch_magnitude: ~3-4M net over the swipe (well below FB1); low confidence due to reload timing

fb3_batch.batch_attributable: ~17M after removing ~10M/s sustained baseline; large, same order as FB1

requirement_4_other_fb_batches.conclusion: Batches recur at every FB-start. FB1 (~25.3M) is the LARGEST (longest ~14s accumulation window). This DIRECTLY contradicts the task premise that FB1 is the smallest batch.

candidate_single_explosion_values.note: Isolated (non-consolidated) explosion-class plain/white popups seen mid-FB, low confidence they are single rockets vs partial reads:
candidate_single_explosion_values.if_representative: 25.3M / ~1.2M ~= 21 instances (upper bound); 25.3M / 4.5M ~= 6 (lower bound). True per-rocket value remains UNMEASURED - needs the isolated slow-mo recording (wishlist #1).

open_ambiguities: The 25.3M FB-start popup could be Rapi's accumulated-rocket BATCH or her burst-skill nuke (both land at FB start); this footage cannot separate them. Either way it is Rapi's dominant FB-start damage event and renders plain/non-core.
Explosion CORE fraction is not truly settled: the consolidated batch renders non-core, but individual rockets could still core - unresolvable while consolidated.
Per-rocket explosion value and exact rockets/FB remain UNMEASURED (consolidation + overlap). The isolated single-explosion-batch slow-mo recording (recording-wishlist #1) is still required.
measurement_discipline: Measurement only. No engine/sim/override edits. Values are OCR-by-eye from frame-stepped native-resolution crops; damage-counter deltas used as cross-checks (team-total, so not Rapi-isolating).
